creator: sdav2@yahoo.com
text:
Yes.  Two days ago.  No reply yet.
What confuses me most is that I can't be the only
person experiencing this so...
what do other people do whan a port doesn't work?

I do a cvsup and then a make.
If it fails then I usually file a bug report on the
ports category.

Is this correct?

--- Chris Faulhaber <jedgar@fxp.org> wrote:
> On Tue, Dec 19, 2000 at 09:42:27AM -0800, stevedav@pacbell.net wrote:
> > 
> > >Number:         23646
> > >Category:       ports
> > >Synopsis:       Staroffice 5.2 fails to install on FreeBSD
> 4.1.1-RELEASE
> >
> *snip*
> > 
> > glibc version: 2.1.2
> > /usr/ports/editors/staroffice52/work/tmp/sv001.tmp/setup.bin: error
> in loading shared libraries: libvos1GCC.so: cannot open shared object
> file: No such file or directory
> > *** Error code 1 (ignored)
> > 
> > An error occured during StarOffice5.2 install. Please send a mail
> with debug-output and
> > some information about your FreeBSD-environment to mb@imp.ch.
> Thanks.
> > 
> 
> And have you sent the above email to the maintainer?
> 
> -- 
> Chris D. Faulhaber - jedgar@fxp.org - jedgar@FreeBSD.org
